jokes.text: Who'd like a banger in the mouth? Oh, right. I forgot. Here in the States you call it a sausage in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Tobias as Mrs. Featherbottom using British terminology
jokes.punchline: The unintentionally sexual double entendre of 'banger in the mouth'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Tobias's obliviousness to sexual innuendo while in drag creates multilayered humor. The correction makes it worse, not better.

jokes.text: We just call it a sausage.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to correct his sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact correction that misses the real issue
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that extends the previous joke without overselling it.

jokes.text: The family, of course, knew it was Tobias... but the house had never been cleaner.
jokes.setup: Family's awareness of Tobias's terrible disguise
jokes.punchline: They're enabling it because he's actually useful as a housekeeper
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the obvious 'they don't know' trope with practical family logic.

jokes.text: The premier- of Canada. He's going out with my gym teacher.
jokes.setup: Lindsay talking about a premiere
jokes.punchline: She's actually talking about the Prime Minister of Canada dating her gym teacher
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - self-absorbed and confused about basic facts, delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: Yeah. I looked that up. It's a ninth grade history textbook.
jokes.setup: Maeby asking for coverage on what sounds like a film
jokes.punchline: It's literally just a textbook
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for Maeby's con, revealing her incompetence in her fake job.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, the job was wreaking havoc with her language. Maybe we just need a new- [Bleeps] housekeeper.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Maeby's language is being affected by her studio job
jokes.punchline: She immediately proves it by swearing while talking about housekeeping
jokes.explanation: Good immediate demonstration of the narrator's point, showing rather than just telling.

jokes.text: Having my day ruined with whatever you're about to ask me to do.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking what Michael is doing tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Michael's preemptively pessimistic response
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - resigned acceptance of family dysfunction delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: He is aware that he's not married to you, right?
jokes.setup: Oscar wanting to throw Lucille an anniversary party
jokes.punchline: Michael pointing out the obvious flaw in this plan
jokes.explanation: Michael as voice of reason in an absurd situation - economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Not now. It's my time of the month. Not now. I don't want to risk having a baby. It could kill me.
jokes.setup: Oscar perceiving Lucille is pulling away
jokes.punchline: Lucille's ridiculous excuses for avoiding intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - dramatic, self-serving excuses delivered with complete conviction.

jokes.text: Well, he didn't return when I lost my hand. But you- you not only embrace it... but I saw you kissing it while you thought I was sleeping.
jokes.setup: Buster explaining George Sr. isn't around
jokes.punchline: The disturbing revelation about Oscar's reaction to his missing hand
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable revelation that adds new disturbing layers to Oscar and Buster's relationship.

jokes.text: Can I tell you somethin', my man? You are one cool- [Bleeps] Speakin' of mothers, let me give that oatmeal some brown sugar.
jokes.setup: Gob's puppet Franklin talking to him
jokes.punchline: Franklin making inappropriate sexual comments about audience members
jokes.explanation: Franklin's inappropriate commentary creates perfect cringe comedy and explains later consequences.

jokes.text: Gob, weren't you also mercilessly beaten outside of a club in Torrance for that act? He also said some things that African-Americany wasn't ready to hear either.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Franklin's inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casual mention of Gob being beaten up, plus her invented word 'African-Americany'
jokes.explanation: Peak Lucille - casual cruelty combined with accidental racism through trying to be politically correct.

jokes.text: As it turned out, he got too serious with the teddy bear.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining she put a surveillance teddy bear in Buster's room
jokes.punchline: Buster developed romantic feelings for the teddy bear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Arrested Development absurdity - takes a throwaway surveillance gag to its most disturbing logical conclusion with Buster's psychology.

jokes.text: I just don't want him to point out my cracker ass in front of Ann. Imagine what he'd say about her.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about Franklin at the party
jokes.punchline: His innocent implication that Ann is even more mockable than he is
jokes.explanation: George Michael's innocent cruelty about Ann, delivered without awareness of what he's saying.

jokes.text: In fact, Lindsay was currently wearing it on her middle toe, roast beef.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming George Sr. would never part with something precious
jokes.punchline: She's wearing the ring on her toe and eating roast beef
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual contradiction of Lindsay's statement, with the absurd detail of toe-wearing adding character specificity.

jokes.text: Well, that's the first I've heard of this. Well, then who said 'Her?' Me. That was-That was me. But, um, her?
jokes.setup: George Michael talking about pre-engagement while George Sr. is hidden
jokes.punchline: Michael trying to cover for his father's involuntary reaction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the hidden father subplot, with Michael's awkward covering creating suspense.

jokes.text: Actually, you tried to put a banger in my mouth this morning.
jokes.setup: Tobias as Mrs. Featherbottom claiming not to have seen George Michael
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent reference to the earlier sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that gains new meaning through George Michael's innocence about the innuendo.

jokes.text: N-N-N- Y-Yes.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking for a ride to Ann's
jokes.punchline: Michael's involuntary attempt to say no before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Michael's internal struggle externalized in a perfectly timed stammer.

jokes.text: Get your loser hand out of my ass.
jokes.setup: Gob visiting Franklin
jokes.punchline: George Sr. is hiding inside Franklin, creating uncomfortable physical contact with Gob
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal of George Sr.'s hiding place through the most uncomfortable possible interaction.

jokes.text: Never too late to start.
jokes.setup: Gob saying he's going to make his father proud
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s casual acknowledgment that Gob has never made him proud
jokes.explanation: Devastating parental cruelty delivered with casual indifference - peak George Sr.

jokes.text: I don't go religiously. I mean, I'm not a nut about it. Because there is no such thing as a nut when it comes to religion, Pastor.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to connect with Pastor Veal about church
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempts to be relatable backfire spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to be agreeable but making it worse with each word.

jokes.text: Next you're gonna say how Ann and I can be sisters.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Mrs. Veal looking young
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Veal's bitter preemptive response to what she expects him to say
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Mrs. Veal's insecurities and the Ann appearance running gag.

jokes.text: Who can blame you? You gotta lock that down.
jokes.setup: Discussing the Veals getting serious at George Michael's age
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Veal's blunt assessment of her own husband's appeal
jokes.explanation: Mrs. Veal's surprising directness about her marriage creates unexpected character depth.

jokes.text: We shan't be telling your mother this, shan't we?
jokes.setup: Tobias attempting a dramatic entrance as Mrs. Featherbottom
jokes.punchline: His failed entrance and injured plea for secrecy
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias physical comedy combined with his desperate need to maintain the Mrs. Featherbottom illusion.

jokes.text: Well, Ann's got a great deal of mass.
jokes.setup: George Michael mentioning Ann went to mass
jokes.punchline: Michael's wordplay suggesting Ann is heavy
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that advances Michael's dislike of Ann.

jokes.text: Fifteen-year-old. Marry me!
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Maeby's handwriting
jokes.punchline: Maeby using her fake age to deflect a marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Maeby's age con as a solution to unwanted romantic attention.

jokes.text: Babysit me.
jokes.setup: Guard commenting she seems old for writing to movie stars
jokes.punchline: Maeby's increasingly absurd deflection using her fake young age
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical absurdist humor that shows Maeby's quick thinking.

jokes.text: Dad escaped from prison. Did you not know that?
jokes.setup: Mrs. Veal eager to meet Michael's husband
jokes.punchline: Michael casually mentioning his father is an escaped convict
jokes.explanation: Michael's plan to shock the Veals executed with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: I ain't your daddy! How's it goin', brother?
jokes.setup: Police stopping the limo
jokes.punchline: George Sr. trying to maintain cover followed by Franklin's casual greeting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of George Sr.'s panic and Franklin's laid-back attitude.

jokes.text: Bring her in here, knock her out with this ether, and you stuff her into this garment bag.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out how to get Lucille to the church
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s immediate escalation to kidnapping with specific criminal instructions
jokes.explanation: Peak George Sr. criminality - treating kidnapping as a casual solution with professional expertise.

jokes.text: Put the ether on the puppet's lips. Have the puppet kiss her. I ain't kissin' that old bitch. That's my wife, you bastard!
jokes.setup: George Sr.'s kidnapping plan getting more elaborate
jokes.punchline: The absurd ether-puppet plan, Franklin's refusal, and Gob's outrage
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation of absurdity - ether-soaked puppet assault becomes a three-way argument about Lucille.

jokes.text: The mere fact that you're laughing about marriage... means that you're not ready, okay?
jokes.setup: George Michael joking about pre-pre-engagement
jokes.punchline: Michael using George Michael's joy against his own proposal
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation showing Michael's desperation to stop the engagement. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Maybe I should ask if she wants to get pre-pre-engaged. Add another-
jokes.setup: Michael discouraging the pre-engagement
jokes.punchline: George Michael's logic that more 'pre's would make it less serious
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael logic - innocent but completely missing the point.

jokes.text: Oh, who let this little black- [Bleeps]
jokes.setup: Franklin appearing at the party
jokes.punchline: Lucille's immediate racist reaction
jokes.explanation: Typical Lucille racism, establishing her character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I want to please you secularly.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Veal's attraction to Michael's 'secular' lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Using 'secularly' as if it's a sexual position or technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Mrs. Veal's repressed sexuality and religious confusion.

jokes.text: I was surprised because of Ann. Well- Why because of Ann? Because of an old wives' tale.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Veal asking why Michael called her beautiful because of Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael's diplomatic reference to the old wives' tale about daughters resembling mothers
jokes.explanation: Clever way for Michael to insult Ann while seeming to compliment Mrs. Veal.

jokes.text: Well, this was before he saw you pounding that sweet piece of veal.
jokes.setup: Michael asking where George Michael went
jokes.punchline: Tobias's unintentionally sexual description using the family name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias double entendre - completely unintentional sexual innuendo using the Veal family name.

jokes.text: Well, this was before he saw you pounding that sweet piece of veal.
jokes.setup: George Michael's engagement decision timing
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre using 'veal' as meat/name pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting the family name to sexual innuendo. Good structure and character work from Tobias.

jokes.text: Her? Does she look old enough to play Topher Grace's mom?
jokes.setup: Introduction of Mrs. Veal as 'the hottie'
jokes.punchline: Maeby's specific Hollywood reference to judge attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby line - applying Hollywood logic to real life with specific actor reference.

jokes.text: I had this shipped over from 'Blackstool.' It's what I used to drive the Roger Moores about in.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his rental car
jokes.punchline: His fake British references and plural 'Roger Moores'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias fabrication with perfect character-specific details and grammatical error.

jokes.text: What am I doing in a Nordstrom bag?
jokes.setup: Lucille waking up after being kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Her concern is about the brand of bag, not being kidnapped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - her priorities are completely warped, caring more about brand than kidnapping. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Oh, man. I can't wait to get married.
jokes.setup: Pastor Veal reading extremely sexual wedding vows
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent enthusiasm about marriage after hearing graphic content
jokes.explanation: Perfect innocent misunderstanding - George Michael's pure reaction to inappropriate content.

jokes.text: I'll always be here for you to rest your ankles upon my shoulders.
jokes.setup: Pastor Veal continuing his inappropriate wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specificity of 'ankles on shoulders' in wedding vows
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and inappropriate detail that makes the religious hypocrisy even more absurd.

jokes.text: 'I'll always be here for you to rest your ankles upon my shoulders.'
jokes.setup: Pastor continuing to read wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Sexual position reference disguised as romantic poetry
jokes.explanation: Continuation of inappropriate vows theme with thinly veiled sexual position reference.

jokes.text: But they did think alike... and neither could land a blow.
jokes.setup: Oscar charging at George Sr. to fight for Lucille
jokes.punchline: Their identical thinking prevents them from hitting each other
jokes.explanation: Genius visual gag that perfectly uses the twin brother concept - they're too similar to fight effectively.

jokes.text: You really should lock that down.
jokes.setup: Pastor Veal angry about his wife kissing Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael using the pastor's own crude language back at him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Michael throwing the pastor's own words back at him. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Put your hands up, or we'll take that as a sign of aggression against us. They're not up! He's aggressive!
jokes.setup: Police confronting Tobias as Mrs. Featherbottom
jokes.punchline: Impossible police logic that guarantees arrest regardless of compliance
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence gag with catch-22 logic, though less character-specific than show's best.

jokes.text: Oh, no. My wig seems to have run off. On George Sr., as it turned out.
jokes.setup: Tobias noticing his wig is missing
jokes.punchline: The wig is now on George Sr., creating a visual reveal
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff that creates absurd image while advancing the plot chaos.

jokes.text: Let's alternate hot and cool on that.
jokes.setup: Oscar claiming he broke his ankle
jokes.punchline: Lucille referencing the inappropriate wedding vows they just heard
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Lucille was paying attention and finds the inappropriate content appealing.

jokes.text: Let's alternate hot and cool on that.
jokes.setup: George Sr. saying he broke his ankle
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Veal referencing the sexual wedding vows for medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mrs. Veal's continued fixation on the sexual content from earlier.

jokes.text: You must teach me, George Michael. You must teach me the ways of the secular flesh.
jokes.setup: Michael giving permission for George Michael and Ann to get to know each other
jokes.punchline: Ann's dramatic religious interpretation as sexual instruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment for Ann - her repressed religious upbringing creates dramatic misinterpretation of innocent advice.

jokes.text: You must teach me, George Michael. You must teach me the ways of the secular flesh.
jokes.setup: Michael giving permission for George Michael and Ann to get to know each other better
jokes.punchline: Ann interpreting this as permission for sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation - Ann's repressed sexuality exploding out with religious language. Great escalation from innocent to sexual.

jokes.text: Drug dog's got something. Get him, Bennett! I don't want no part of your tight-ass country club... you freak bitch!
jokes.setup: Police drug dog reacting to Buster's hook replacement
jokes.punchline: Franklin puppet cursing at police and country club in one outburst
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Franklin's inappropriate behavior, though feels more like setup for next episode than strong standalone comedy.

jokes.text: I don't want no part of your tight-ass country club... you freak bitch!
jokes.setup: Preview of next episode with Buster's hook replacement
jokes.punchline: Franklin's aggressive response to country club exclusion
jokes.explanation: Good teaser that shows Franklin's personality extending to Buster's replacement hand.
